summary: K1.6 NON-PORT CONFIRMED: CocoIndex has query metadata and backend target/index declarations, but no router, intent classifier, RRF fusion, channel selector, or entity-density query orchestration to port. K2.1: mcp__ is host-added, not MCP-standard; Claude documents mcp__<server>__<tool>, Gemini documents mcp_<server>_<tool>, OpenCode documents server-name-prefixed tool globs, and Codex docs confirm mcp_servers registration plus raw tool filters without documenting a Claude-style display prefix.

claim: The only top-level CocoIndex Python query file found is query_handler.py, and it defines query result metadata/output wrappers rather than retrieval or routing logic.
evidence: query_handler.py:9-18 defines result field metadata for embedding/score
query_handler.py:29-37 defines QueryInfo with optional query embedding
query_handler.py:42-52 defines QueryOutput as results plus query_info

claim: CocoIndex connector vector support is backend-local target/index declaration, not cross-backend query intelligence.
evidence: postgres/_target.py:394-400 maps pgvector operator classes
postgres/_target.py:420-460 applies vector index create/drop actions
postgres/_target.py:1211-1248 declares a pgvector index target state
qdrant/_target.py:1-7 says the module manages collections and point upserts/deletes
qdrant/_target.py:520-585 declares and mounts Qdrant collection targets

claim: CocoIndex statediff is adjacent reconciliation machinery upstream of retrieval, not a router, classifier, reranker, or fusion layer.
evidence: statediff.py:1-18 describes desired/prior state diffing into write actions
statediff.py:52 defines insert/upsert/replace/delete actions
statediff.py:149-186 implements the action decision tree
statediff.py:189-251 computes composite sub-state transitions

claim: Our query intelligence is a TypeScript host concern: five channels, tier routing, intent preservation, graph preservation, entity-density routing, and query-plan assembly live in query-router.ts.
evidence: query-router.ts:35-37 defines vector/fts/bm25/graph/degree channels
query-router.ts:98-108 maps complexity tiers to channel subsets
query-router.ts:149-170 preserves BM25 for authority-focused queries
query-router.ts:206-255 preserves graph/degree for intent or entity-density
query-router.ts:325-447 implements routeQuery

claim: The MCP protocol does not standardize mcp__<server>__<tool>; it scopes tool-name uniqueness to a server and leaves aggregate disambiguation to clients/proxies.
evidence: MCP tools spec lines 300-310: raw tool-name constraints, unique within server, aggregators should implement disambiguation such as prefixing

claim: Our MCP server defines raw tool names without host prefixes; the visible prefix is added by the client host after tool discovery.
evidence: tool-schemas.ts:32-39 defines ToolDefinition.name as a raw string
tool-schemas.ts:47-56 defines memory_context and memory_search without server prefixes

claim: The server-name segment spec_kit_memory comes from the runtime config key in each host, not from server tool definitions.
evidence: .claude/mcp.json:1-15 registers mcpServers.spec_kit_memory
opencode.json:10-24 registers mcp.spec_kit_memory
.codex/config.toml:5-13 registers mcp_servers.spec_kit_memory
.gemini/settings.json:17-49 registers mcpServers.spec_kit_memory

claim: Cross-runtime displayed tool-name shapes differ: Claude documents mcp__<server>__<tool>, Gemini documents mcp_<server>_<tool>, OpenCode documents server-name-prefixed tool globs, and Codex docs found here do not document a Claude-style composed display name.
evidence: Claude docs lines 200-202 define mcp__<server-name>__<tool-name>
Gemini docs lines 624-633 define mcp_{serverName}_{toolName} and warn against underscores in server names
OpenCode docs lines 570-582 show server-name-prefixed tool globs
Codex docs lines 622-653 define [mcp_servers.<server-name>] registration and lines 672-678 define raw enabled_tools/disabled_tools filters
